topic: Someone receives flowers with no card attached
raw_output:
[story 1]

The bouquet arrived this morning, a vibrant, almost aggressively cheerful arrangement of sunflowers and deep purple irises. I found them sitting on my kitchen counter, untouched, like a bizarre, unsolicited offering. My jaw tightened as I picked up the vase, the stems cool against my fingers. There was no note, no inscription, just the flowers, starkly presented. I stared at the arrangement, my brow furrowing into a deep crease. Who sends something so extravagant without even a single word? I walked to the window, letting the morning light reflect off the glossy petals, feeling a distinct prickle of irritation crawl up my neck. It felt like a deliberate snub, a silent commentary on my existence.

[story 2]

When the delivery person handed over the wrapped package, my hands felt clammy. Inside, nestled in crinkled tissue paper, were roses—a shade of crimson so intense it seemed almost painful to look at. The silence in the hallway as I took the box was heavy, weighted with unspoken expectation. I carried it back to my apartment, placing it on the entryway table with exaggerated care. I circled the arrangement twice, my gaze sharp and assessing. My lips pressed into a thin, straight line. I wanted to ask who it was, to understand the motivation behind such a grand gesture, but the absence of any accompanying message left me with a sour, unsettled feeling churning in my stomach.

[story 3]

I saw them sitting by the front door—a cascade of lilies and delicate white blooms. They were beautiful, undeniably so, but the lack of any accompanying paper felt like a deliberate erasure. I hesitated for a long moment before reaching out. My fingers brushed the cool silk of a petal, and a sudden tension tightened my shoulders. I let out a small, sharp exhale, a sound more of disbelief than surprise. I straightened up quickly, turning away from the flowers as if they were suddenly too much to bear. I began tidying up the entryway with unnecessary force, my movements jerky, trying to shake off the strange sense of being addressed without being acknowledged.
